signals.label: Functional status (real-world)
signals.evidence_span: I still do my garden every morning, the tomatoes are coming in. I cook for myself, I drive to church and the store.

signals.label: Treatment intent
signals.evidence_span: I want the one that gives me the best shot. I didn't drive all this way to go easy.

signals.label: Distance to cellular-therapy center
signals.evidence_span: About an hour and a half, but my daughter drove, so it wasn't bad.

signals.label: Caregiver reliability
signals.evidence_span: I'm Carla, her daughter. I come to the important ones.

signals.label: Gentler vs active preference
signals.evidence_span: Then let's do it. The strong one.

rationale.text: Age 86, transplant-ineligible, prior R-CHOP now refractory: a chart-only read down-triages toward a gentler, chemo-light, largely outpatient regimen (SUNMO).
Highly active bispecific + chemotherapy combination for transplant-ineligible R/R DLBCL (EPCORE NHL-2).

off_guideline.boundary: Only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved, and only at 3L+. The GemOx combination and 2L use are off-label.
off_guideline.tradeoff: Trades on-label status for a prospectively-supported, deeper response in a fit, transplant-ineligible patient not pursuing CAR-T.

rationale.text: Excluded: L-MIND excluded primary-refractory disease, so there is no efficacy evidence here. The exclusion is by disease biology — not age, not transplant-ineligibility.
Deprioritized on a composite of comorbidity + distance + caregiver, pending the room. Not an age rule.
Robust performance status (gardens daily, independent) and explicit active goals argue against under-treating a phenotypically fit patient — age is not the decision.
Patient wants the most active option and is not pursuing CAR-T; a highly active bispecific + chemo combination fits.
Bispecific ramp-up (cycle 1) delivered at the center, then handed back to the local oncologist — makes a distant patient treatable.

off_guideline.boundary: Only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved, and only at 3L+. The GemOx combination and 2L use are off-label.
off_guideline.tradeoff: Off strict guideline for this line, chosen deliberately for a robust, fit, transplant-ineligible patient to avoid under-treatment; grounded in EPCORE NHL-2 (ORR 85%, CR 61%).

rationale.text: A legitimate gentler option, but it can under-treat a phenotypically fit patient with primary-refractory disease.
Excluded: primary-refractory disease was an explicit L-MIND exclusion, so there is no efficacy evidence. Not because of age, and not because she is transplant-ineligible.

Excluded on a composite: 90-minute distance from the certified center plus limited caregiver availability make the sustained CRS/ICANS monitoring window unworkable. This is the failing composite — never a numeric age cutoff.

rule_checks.message: Tafasitamab-lenalidomide excluded in primary-refractory disease (L-MIND excluded primary-refractory). Exclusion is by biology, not age or transplant-ineligibility.
Epcoritamab + GemOx is off-label in 2L (only epcoritamab monotherapy is approved, 3L+). Boundary named, grounded in EPCORE NHL-2; clinician may proceed.
Do not down-rank an active regimen solely on age/biology when disease is primary-refractory; under-treating a phenotypically fit patient is the greater risk.
Bispecific for a distant patient: cycle-1 ramp-up assigned to the center, hand-back after the ramp-up window. Highest-risk doses stay at the center.
CAR-T ineligibility represented as a failing fitness+logistics+caregiver composite, never a numeric age cutoff.

groundings.claim_text: L-MIND excluded primary-refractory disease; no efficacy evidence for tafa-len there.
Epcoritamab + GemOx (EPCORE NHL-2) is an off-label 2L combination with high CMR in transplant-ineligible R/R DLBCL.
Refractory DLBCL has poor prognosis; under-treating a fit patient risks a foreseeable poor outcome.
No numeric age cutoff for CAR-T; eligibility is a fitness+logistics composite.
groundings.quote: no label-based upper age limit for CAR-T
